translated: When that woman was trembling with fear and what she had done, she then came and knelt before Jesus and told him all that she had done, confessing.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Yoomyooma ka bipumbila maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna: - u daa nyɛ la yuuma piiya ni ayi biiya. Nŋɔ maa daa niŋi ba alahazibu ni sufupeelli pam.
translated: Yoomyooma and the children had gathered, sat, waited, and stood. He was a twelve-year-old child. This one had done a miracle for them with great joy.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Ka u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, “N nin ti i nyin boori seelli kam zaa halli di yi nyɛ n naam tingbaŋŋu maa purigri.”
translated: And he spoke, saying to him, "I will give you whatever you desire, even half of my kingdom.

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ka ba daa doaai n‑kpɛ ba nyariŋŋu la n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ba kɔkɔa.
translated: And when they had finished and entered their house, they found a small room in it and hid.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Ka saha ŋɔn daa pun yuui ka yuŋŋu zibri maa, u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ na nti yɛli u, “Saha pun gaari, ka kpa wa laa n‑nyɛ la luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni.
translated: When the day was already far spent and evening was drawing near, his disciples came to him and said, "It is already late, and this place where we are is a desolate place.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya tim ba bunseelli ka ba di.” Ka ba labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i boori la ti kyaŋŋi nti da ligri pam paanu na n‑diisi ba, bee? Ay! - ti ligri ku paai lala!”
translated: And Jesus answered and said to them, "You give them something to eat." And they answered him, "Are we to go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread to feed them? Oh! Our money won't be enough for that!

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Di saha ka Yisa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, ba kyɛ ka niriba maa puri ba mansi n‑zinni mɔmaasa la zugu.
translated: When Jesus had finished telling his disciples, they allowed the people to lay their hands on their heads.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa, sahanseaa u mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa, Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni la n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri sa n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.
translated: After this, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead of him to the other side, to the town called Bethsaida, with the five loaves and two fish that remained after feeding

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti puusi Naawunni.
translated: When he had finished praying for them, he went up to the mountain to worship God.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Ban daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni la, bɛni niriba maa daa baŋŋi ya'abi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u,
translated: When he performed that miracle, those people truly knew that it was Jesus.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: “Yarima muna - ya za'asiri Naawunni soori, ni ya ti dɔli ya mammaŋŋa kaari maa: yam nla, bee?
translated: Yarima, we believe he will follow God's path and obey all your advice, won't he?

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Anabi Aizaaya daa pun mari yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn daa bugi n‑yɛli ya birikɔ'anu dima yɛla seem la; ka daa sɔbi, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- ‘Niriba wa gyeemni ma la ni ba nangbana. Ba sufu mun yi n saani Ka kpalim n‑wɔ'a;
translated: And Jesus answered and said to them, "The prophet Isaiah had already prophesied about you hypocrites, as it is written: God says, 'These people honor me with their lips. But their hearts are far from me;

ref: You won't let those people help their parents.
text: ka mɔŋŋi u ba ni u ma dizugu, ni, u ka taalli.
translated: And he found his father and his mother together, and he greeted them.

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.”
translated: Sɔkanŋɔ is a teacher who teaches people and always glorifies God. And there are many things he does that you should also do.

ref: The food that you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Seelli ka ni din nin kpɛ ninsaala puuni ka kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira puuni na n kyɛ ka u leebri da'ari daana.”
translated: What goes into a person does not make them unclean before God. But what comes out of a person, that makes them unclean.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”
translated: All evil desires and sin come from within a person and make him a sinner.

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Yisa daa yisigi n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya tiŋŋa la. U daa kpɛ yiri ni ka bu boori ni sɔsɔ baŋŋi, ni, u bɛ bɛni. Amaa u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai.
translated: Jesus had arrived, tired and thirsty, in a town called Taaya. He entered a house, wanting to rest, for he was tired. But he could not rest.

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Mboo! Labsigu seaa nyin labsi wa zugu, kur labma n‑kuli; i nin paai n‑nya ka arizinni maa yiya, ka kyɛ i bipuŋŋa maa!”
translated: And Jesus said to him, “Behold! The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few; therefore pray to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest!

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa u kɔkɔ ka kyɛ zama maa, ka zaŋŋi u nubisi n‑tum doo maa tuba ni; ka tusi nantoori n‑zaŋŋi n‑siisi doo maa zulimni.
translated: And Jesus used his power to heal the sick people, and he took his hands to touch the man's face; and he spat saliva on the ground and took it to smear on the man's eyes.
